40.reason: [Function getWord does not return a value in all control paths. When the first while loop exits due to isalpha(c) being true and then the second while loop processes letters until non-alpha, it sets w[i]='\0' and checks strlen(w)>0, but if strlen(w)==0 (which shouldn't happen logically but compiler sees missing return), it falls through without returning. Also, the function signature expects to return int for success/failure/EOF, but logic is inconsistent with reference.]

111.reason: [The program does not check if fopen succeeded. If "article.txt" doesn't exist, in will be NULL and subsequent fgetc calls cause undefined behavior. Also, the getWord function has flawed logic: the first while loop skips non-alpha chars, but when it finds an alpha char, it breaks and enters the second loop鈥攂ut the variable 'c' holds the first alpha char, yet the second loop condition is while(isalpha(c)), which is true, but then immediately does c=fgetc(bfp), losing the first character. This causes the first letter of every word to be dropped. Additionally, the function may read past EOF without checking after the second fgetc.]
